title: Tools System â€” GitHub, Gmail, Web Search

tasks.description: Add toolCredentials table and expand tools schema to support OAuth metadata and tool type
steps: Edit `packages/db/src/schema/tools.ts`: add `toolCredentials` table with columns: id (UUID PK), toolId (FKâ†’tools, unique â€” one credential per tool since per-workspace), accessToken (text, encrypted), refreshToken (text, nullable), tokenExpiresAt (timestamp, nullable), scope (text, nullable), createdAt, updatedAt
Add `type` column to `tools` table: varchar(20) for 'oauth' | 'api_key' | 'builtin', defaulting to 'builtin'
Add `slug` column to `tools` table: varchar(50) unique, not null â€” machine identifier (e.g., 'github', 'gmail', 'web_search')
Add `oauthProvider` column to `tools` table: varchar(50) nullable â€” identifies the OAuth provider
Add `requiredScopes` column to `tools` table: jsonb string[] default [] â€” OAuth scopes needed
Export new table + types (ToolCredentialRow, NewToolCredential) from `packages/db/src/schema/tools.ts`, `packages/db/src/schema/index.ts`, and `packages/db/src/index.ts`
Generate migration: `cd packages/db && pnpm drizzle-kit generate`

tasks.description: Expand shared Tool types to include OAuth status, tool type, and credential info for frontend consumption
steps: Edit `packages/shared/src/types/tool.ts`: add `slug`, `type` ('oauth'|'api_key'|'builtin'), `oauthProvider`, `requiredScopes` to Tool interface
Add `ToolWithStatus` interface extending Tool with `connected: boolean` (credential exists and valid)
Add `ToolCredentialStatus` interface: { connected: boolean, scope?: string, expiresAt?: string }
Add `TOOL_SLUGS` const: { GITHUB: 'github', GMAIL: 'gmail', WEB_SEARCH: 'web_search' } as const
Export all new types from `packages/shared/src/types/index.ts`

tasks.description: Create seed script and npm command to populate the 3 initial tools in the database
steps: Create `packages/db/src/seed.ts`: import db client + tools table, insert 3 tool rows

GitHub: { slug: 'github', name: 'GitHub', description: 'Search repositories, list issues, and read file contents from GitHub.', type: 'oauth', icon: 'github', category: 'development', oauthProvider: 'github', requiredScopes: ['repo', 'read:user'] }

Gmail: { slug: 'gmail', name: 'Gmail', description: 'Send emails via your connected Gmail account.', type: 'oauth', icon: 'mail', category: 'communication', oauthProvider: 'google', requiredScopes: ['https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.send'] }
Web Search: { slug: 'web_search', name: 'Web Search', description: 'Search the web for real-time information using Tavily.', type: 'api_key', icon: 'search', category: 'research' }
Use upsert (ON CONFLICT ON slug DO UPDATE) so seed is idempotent
Add `"db:seed": "tsx src/seed.ts"` script to `packages/db/package.json`
Update root `package.json` `db:setup` script from `pnpm db:migrate` to `pnpm db:migrate && pnpm --filter @siestai/db db:seed`

tasks.description: Add env vars for tool OAuth providers (GitHub OAuth app, Google Tools OAuth, Tavily API key, Agent Tool Secret)
steps: Edit `.env.staging.example`: add GITHUB_TOOLS_CLIENT_ID, GITHUB_TOOLS_CLIENT_SECRET, GOOGLE_TOOLS_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_TOOLS_CLIENT_SECRET, TAVILY_API_KEY, AGENT_TOOL_SECRET
Add comment blocks explaining these are separate from login OAuth: '# Tool OAuth (separate from user login OAuth)'
Note: Do NOT add validation in app.module.ts that crashes â€” these are optional until tools are configured

tasks.description: Build OAuthService for handling OAuth flows â€” authorization URL generation, token exchange, refresh, and encryption
steps: Create `apps/api/src/tools/oauth.service.ts`

Implement `encrypt(plaintext: string): string` and `decrypt(ciphertext: string): string` using Node crypto aes-256-gcm with BETTER_AUTH_SECRET as key (derive via scrypt or sha256). Store as `iv:authTag:ciphertext` hex string
Implement `getAuthorizationUrl(toolSlug: string): string` â€” generates OAuth redirect URL with state param (HMAC-signed toolSlug). Callback URL: `{BACKEND_URL}/tools/oauth/callback`
Implement `exchangeCode(toolSlug: string, code: string): Promise<{ accessToken, refreshToken?, expiresIn? }>` â€” exchanges auth code for tokens via POST to token URL
Implement `refreshToken(toolId: string): Promise<string>` â€” reads encrypted refresh token from DB, calls token URL, updates DB with new tokens
Implement `getValidToken(toolSlug: string): Promise<string>` â€” reads credential, checks expiry, auto-refreshes if needed, returns decrypted access token
GitHub OAuth: authorize `https://github.com/login/oauth/authorize`, token `https://github.com/login/oauth/access_token` (set Accept: application/json header)
Google/Gmail OAuth: authorize `https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth`, token `https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token`, include `access_type=offline&prompt=consent` for refresh token
Inject ConfigService for GITHUB_TOOLS_CLIENT_ID/SECRET, GOOGLE_TOOLS_CLIENT_ID/SECRET, BETTER_AUTH_SECRET, BACKEND_URL

tasks.description: Extend ToolsController with OAuth endpoints â€” initiate flow, callback, status, and disconnect
steps: Add `GET /tools/:slug/oauth/connect` â€” calls oauthService.getAuthorizationUrl(slug), returns 302 redirect to provider

Add `GET /tools/oauth/callback` (@AllowAnonymous) â€” reads `code` + `state` query params, validates state HMAC, calls oauthService.exchangeCode(), stores via toolsService.saveToolCredential(), redirects to `{FRONTEND_URL}/tools/callback?connected={slug}`
Add `GET /tools/:slug/oauth/status` â€” returns { connected: boolean, scope?: string, expiresAt?: string }
Add `DELETE /tools/:slug/oauth/disconnect` â€” calls toolsService.deleteToolCredential(slug)
Add `POST /tools/web_search/configure` â€” body: { apiKey: string }, encrypts and saves to toolCredentials as accessToken
Add `GET /tools/web_search/status` â€” returns { configured: boolean }
Register OAuthService in ToolsModule providers

tasks.description: Extend ToolsService with credential management and tool status queries
steps: Import `toolCredentials` from `@siestai/db` (will be available after task 1 migration)
Add `getToolBySlug(slug: string)` method â€” query tools table by slug column
Add `getToolCredential(toolSlug: string)` â€” join tools + toolCredentials, return row or null
Add `saveToolCredential(toolId: string, data: { accessToken, refreshToken?, tokenExpiresAt?, scope? })` â€” upsert into toolCredentials (ON CONFLICT toolId DO UPDATE)
Add `deleteToolCredential(toolSlug: string)` â€” delete from toolCredentials WHERE toolId = (SELECT id FROM tools WHERE slug = toolSlug)
Add `listToolsWithStatus(): Promise<ToolWithStatus[]>` â€” left join tools with toolCredentials, return tools with `connected` boolean
Add `getAgentToolsWithDefinitions(agentId: string)` â€” join agentTools + tools, return full tool definitions (slug, type, name, description) for all connected tools

tasks.description: Build ToolRegistryService â€” creates Mastra createTool() instances and delegates to executor classes for runtime execution
steps: Create `apps/api/src/tools/tool-registry.service.ts`
Import `createTool` from `@mastra/core/tools` and `z` from `zod`
Implement `buildToolsForAgent(agentId: string): Promise<Record<string, Tool>>` â€” fetches connected tools via ToolsService, builds Mastra tool instances
For each tool slug, construct a createTool() with appropriate id, description, inputSchema (Zod), and execute function
Execute functions should delegate to executor classes (created in tasks 15-17). For now, create stub execute functions that throw 'Not implemented' â€” executors will be wired in phase 7

Define tool schemas: github_search: z.object({query: z.string(), repo: z.string().optional()}), gmail_send: z.object({to: z.string(), subject: z.string(), body: z.string()}), web_search: z.object({query: z.string(), maxResults: z.number().optional().default(5)})
Implement `executeToolAction(toolSlug: string, action: string, params: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<unknown>` â€” used by the /tools/execute proxy endpoint
Inject OAuthService and ToolsService. Register in ToolsModule providers + exports

tasks.description: Inject tools into Mastra agent preview streaming so agents can call tools during text chat
steps: Edit `apps/api/src/agents/preview/agent-preview.service.ts`: inject ToolRegistryService via constructor
When dto.agentId is provided, call `toolRegistry.buildToolsForAgent(dto.agentId)` to get tool map
Pass tools to Agent constructor: `new Agent({ ..., tools: toolMap })` â€” Mastra AgentConfig accepts `tools?: ToolsInput`
Edit `apps/api/src/mastra/runtime.ts`: add optional `tools` parameter to `createRuntimeAgent(record, tools?)` and pass to Agent constructor
Edit `apps/api/src/agents/preview/agent-preview.module.ts`: add `imports: [ToolsModule]` so ToolRegistryService is injectable
Note: AgentPreviewModule currently has no imports array â€” add it

tasks.description: Add tool execution proxy endpoint for voice worker â€” worker calls backend to execute tools instead of running them directly
steps: Create `apps/api/src/tools/dto/execute-tool.dto.ts`: { toolSlug: string, action: string, params: Record<string, unknown> } with class-validator decorators
Add `POST /tools/execute` endpoint to ToolsController, mark @AllowAnonymous
Add a guard: read `X-Agent-Secret` header and compare against ConfigService.get('AGENT_TOOL_SECRET'). Return 403 if mismatch
Delegate to `toolRegistryService.executeToolAction(dto.toolSlug, dto.action, dto.params)`
Return `{ result: unknown }` on success, `{ error: string }` on failure (catch and wrap exceptions)
Add AGENT_TOOL_SECRET to .env.staging.example if not already done in task 4

tasks.description: Update arena session start to include tool definitions in room metadata so voice worker knows which tools are available
steps: Edit `apps/api/src/arena/arena.controller.ts` startSession method
For each native_agent participant with agentId: call `toolsService.getAgentToolsWithDefinitions(p.agentId)` to get connected tool definitions
Add `tools` field to each agent in room metadata: `{ name, instructions, memories, tools: [{ slug, name, description }] }`
Add `toolSecret` field to top-level metadata: `configService.get('AGENT_TOOL_SECRET')` â€” worker uses this to authenticate tool execution calls
Do NOT include OAuth tokens or API keys in metadata â€” worker calls /tools/execute proxy endpoint
Edit `apps/api/src/arena/arena.module.ts`: add ToolsModule to imports array
Inject ToolsService in ArenaController constructor

tasks.description: Update voice worker to parse tool definitions from metadata and execute tools via backend HTTP proxy
steps: Edit `apps/agent/src/arena-agent.ts`: add `tools?: { slug: string, name: string, description: string }[]` to ArenaAgentConfig and ArenaMetadata interfaces

In system prompt construction: if tools are present, add a section: 'You have access to these tools. To use a tool, respond with [TOOL_CALL] toolSlug: {slug}, action: {action}, params: {json}. Wait for the result before continuing.'

Create `apps/agent/src/tool-executor.ts`: class ToolExecutor with method `execute(slug, action, params)` that POSTs to `{backendUrl}/tools/execute` with `X-Agent-Secret` header. Returns parsed result or error string
Edit `apps/agent/src/main.ts`: read `toolSecret` from metadata, instantiate ToolExecutor

In the ConversationItemAdded handler, detect [TOOL_CALL] patterns in assistant output, parse slug/action/params, call ToolExecutor.execute(), inject result back via `session.generateReply({ userInput: '[TOOL_RESULT]: {result}' })`
Add AGENT_TOOL_SECRET to apps/agent .env.local (for local dev)

tasks.description: Extend the existing /tools marketplace page with OAuth connection status, connect/disconnect buttons, and API key configuration
steps: Edit `apps/web/src/app/(app)/tools/page.tsx` (file already exists as a basic listing)
Replace `api.listTools()` call with new `api.listToolsWithStatus()` to get connection status
Add connection status badge to each tool card: 'Connected' (green dot), 'Not Connected' (gray), 'API Key Required' (yellow)
For OAuth tools (type='oauth'): add 'Connect' button that window.location to `{API_URL}/tools/{slug}/oauth/connect`, or 'Disconnect' button if already connected
For API key tools (type='api_key'): add 'Configure' button that opens a dialog/modal to enter the API key, POSTs to `api.configureTool(slug, { apiKey })`
Add `Github` icon from lucide-react to TOOL_ICON_MAP: `github: Github`
Add `api.listToolsWithStatus()`, `api.configureTool(slug, config)`, `api.getToolOAuthStatus(slug)`, `api.disconnectToolOAuth(slug)` methods to `apps/web/src/lib/api.ts`
Note: nav-bar already has /tools link â€” no nav changes needed

tasks.description: Update the agent detail tools section to show OAuth status and gate tool connection on credential availability
steps: Edit `apps/web/src/components/agents/detail/tools-section.tsx`
Replace `api.listTools()` with `api.listToolsWithStatus()` to get ToolWithStatus[] (includes `connected` field)
Add `Github` icon to local TOOL_ICON_MAP: `github: Github` (import from lucide-react)
For each tool in the browser grid: show a status indicator â€” green dot if OAuth/API key configured, orange dot if not
If tool requires OAuth but credential not connected: replace connect button with 'Setup required' link pointing to `/tools`
If tool is configured: allow connecting to agent normally via existing `api.connectAgentTool(agentId, toolId)`
On connected tool pills: add subtle tooltip or subtitle showing tool type (e.g., 'via OAuth')

tasks.description: Implement GitHub tool executor â€” search repos, list issues, read file contents via GitHub REST API
steps: Create `apps/api/src/tools/executors/github.executor.ts`
Export class GitHubExecutor with method `execute(action: string, params: Record<string, unknown>, accessToken: string): Promise<unknown>`
Action 'search_repos': GET `https://api.github.com/search/repositories?q={query}` with Bearer token. Return top 5 results: { items: [{ name, full_name, description, url, stars }] }
Action 'list_issues': GET `https://api.github.com/repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues?state=open&per_page=10`. Return { items: [{ number, title, state, labels, created_at }] }
Action 'get_file': GET `https://api.github.com/repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents/{path}`. Decode base64 content. Return { content: string, path, sha }
Sanitize and truncate all responses to max 4000 chars to avoid bloating LLM context
Wire into ToolRegistryService: replace github stub execute fn with call to GitHubExecutor

tasks.description: Implement Gmail tool executor â€” send email via Gmail API
steps: Create `apps/api/src/tools/executors/gmail.executor.ts`
Export class GmailExecutor with method `execute(action: string, params: Record<string, unknown>, accessToken: string): Promise<unknown>`
Base64url encode the MIME message (replace +â†’-, /â†’_, remove =)
POST `https://gmail.googleapis.com/gmail/v1/users/me/messages/send` with body `{ raw: encodedMessage }` and Bearer token
Return { messageId: string, threadId: string } from Gmail API response
Wire into ToolRegistryService: replace gmail stub execute fn with call to GmailExecutor

tasks.description: Implement Web Search tool executor â€” Tavily search API
steps: Create `apps/api/src/tools/executors/web-search.executor.ts`
Export class WebSearchExecutor with method `execute(action: string, params: Record<string, unknown>, apiKey: string): Promise<unknown>`
Action 'search': POST `https://api.tavily.com/search` with body `{ api_key: apiKey, query, max_results: maxResults || 5, search_depth: 'basic' }`
Return `{ results: [{ title, url, content, score }] }` â€” truncate content to 500 chars per result
Wire into ToolRegistryService: replace web_search stub execute fn with call to WebSearchExecutor

tasks.description: Add OAuth callback landing page on frontend to handle redirect back from OAuth providers
steps: Create `apps/web/src/app/(app)/tools/callback/page.tsx`
Read query params: `?connected={slug}` for success, `?error={message}` for failure
Success: show green CheckCircle2 icon + '{ToolName} connected successfully' + auto-redirect to /tools after 2 seconds using router.push
Error: show red AlertCircle icon + error message + 'Try again' button linking to /tools
Keep it simple â€” use existing shadcn components, match dark theme

tasks.description: End-to-end wiring test â€” verify tool execution proxy and preview integration work correctly
steps: Create `apps/api/src/tools/__tests__/tool-execution.spec.ts`
Test 1: ToolRegistryService.executeToolAction('web_search', 'search', { query: 'test' }) with mocked Tavily response â†’ verify structured result
Test 2: POST /tools/execute with valid AGENT_TOOL_SECRET header â†’ 200 with result. Without header â†’ 403
Test 3: buildToolsForAgent(agentId) for agent with no connected tools â†’ returns empty object
Test 4: OAuthService.encrypt/decrypt round-trip â†’ decrypted matches original
Ensure error handling: missing credential returns clear error message, not a stack trace
